def: s.f. The mountain ebony, Bauhinia variegata (the flowers of which are a delicate vegetable).
s.f. The mountain ebony, Bauhinia variegata (the flowers of which are a delicate vegetable).
s.m. Pricking, piercing, stabbing; puncture, stab (cf. khoṅćā).

ety: prob. a dialec. var. of kūṅćnā, or khoṅćnā
def: v.t. To prick, pierce, puncture, lance, stab.
s.f. A crowd, a dense mass; — adj. Crowded, thick, dense, closely packed, or planted, &c., stuffed together ( = gać-pać).
s.m. corr. of kać-bać, q.v.
s.m. lit. 'Crowded together'; the Pleiades.
s.m. = kaććāpan, q.v.
s.m. The Curcuma zerumbet; also C. pubescens
s.m. Clay; mire, dirt, rubbish, sweepings, bits of strawʼ, &c.; fragments; residuum, sediment, lees.
s.m. A small unripe melon, &c.
s.m. (prov.) corr. of kaćnār, q.v.
s.m. = kaćūmar, q.v.
s.m. Sliced mango or other fruit (for pickling); the pickle of the same
subs: or kaćūmar nikālnā or nikāl-ḍālnā (-kā), 'To slice up for pickling,' to cut to pieces, to make mince-meat (of), to beat to a pulp.

def: s.m. = kaćūmar, q.v.
s.m. The plant or root zedoary, Curcuma zerumbet.
s.m. The plant or root zedoary, Curcuma zerumbet.

ety: kaćūr˚, prob. = S. कच+चूर्णय(ति), fr. चूर्ण
def: v.t. To bruise, crush, triturate ( = kućalnā); to cut up, to slice; to pull or pick to pieces ( = bićūrnā).
v.n. (dialec.) To be grieved or sorry; to be vexed or irritated ( = kaććā honā); to be disgusted (with), to be averse (to), to dislike, loathe, abhor; to shrink (from).
s.f. A minute division of surface in land-measure, the twentieth part of a biswāṅsī, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) Vexation, irritation; dislike, aversion, disgust, abhorrence.
adv. When? ( = kab, q.v.).

ety: contrac. of kada; Pehl. kadak, or katak; Zend kata, rt. kan = S. खन्
def: s.m. A house, habitation; a retreat; a den; a cavern
subs: s.f. Lady of the house; mistress of a family
s.m. Master of the house, &c. ( = kat-ḵẖudā, q.v.)
s.f. A family establishment, housekeeping; marriage ( = kat-ḵẖudāʼī, q.v.)

ety: for A. qadd, inf. n. of قدّ 'to tear lengthwise,' &c.
def: s.m. Height, stature; figure
subs: adj. Of the height of a man; a fathom of
v.n. To bow down; to be bent in figure, to stoop
s.f. Tallness and erectness of stature
adj. Tall and erect of stature
To develop stature or size, to grow big (a child)
s.m. Size and stature; height, stature; figure, build; personal appearance
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَد (qad), from Arabic قَدّ (qadd).

def: s.m. Mud, mire, &c. ( = kādo, q.v.).
adv. When? at what time? — once upon a time; perhaps
subs: (kadā+api], adv. Sometimes, now and then; ever, at all, on any account, in any wise or case (generally followed by a negative)
or kadā-ća, vulg. kadāć, At some time or other, sometimes, once; ever, if (with a negative); should; possibly, probably, perhaps, perchance, if, if perchance, peradventure.

ety: Pehl. kadak, katak; Zend kata, rt. kan = S. khan
def: s.m. Habitation, house; place, &c. (used as last member of comp., e.g. ātash-kada, 'place of fire,' a temple of the Magi; — mai-kada, 'a wine-cellar,' 'a tavern — mātam-kada, 'a house of mourning').
adj. & s.m. (f. -a or -ī), Following evil courses or practices, wicked, abandoned, profligate; — a wicked man, an abandoned character, a profligate.
adv. See s.v. kadā.
s.f. Corroboration; stress; injunction; prohibition (syn. tākīd); — solicitude, care
s.m. A goblet, cup, glass, bowl.

r: kadahiṅ, kad-hiṅ, kadahū, kadahu, kadhū, kadhūṅ, kadhī, kadhīṅ, kad, hū, hī
ety: kad+ emphat. part. hū or hī, qq.v.
def: adv. = kabhī, q.v.).

r: kadahū, kadahu, kadhū, kadahiṅ, kad-hiṅ, kadhūṅ, kadhī, kadhīṅ, kad, hū, hī
ety: kad+ emphat. part. hū or hī, qq.v.
def: adv. = kabhī, q.v.).
adj. Ill-shaped, ill-formed, ugly.
s.m. Bad letter; bad handwriting.
s.m. The plantain or banana tree ( = kadalī); the leaf of the plantain.
s.m. The plantain or banana tree ( = kadalī); the leaf of the plantain.
s.f. The plantain or banana, Musa sapientum, or M. paradisiaca; — a kind of deer; a banner
s.m. = kadamb, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. قدم, inf. n. qadm, 'going before, preceding'
def: s.m. The foot; sole of the foot; a foot's length; a footstep, step, pace; — a going before; merit
subs: To walk fast, to step out
v.n. To put the foot well forward, to step out, to take long steps, to walk fast; to put the foot (beyond, -se); to go or step beyond bounds, to exceed due limits, to transgress; to encroach (on)
adv. Step with step; in the footsteps (of); step by step, slowly, gently, by degrees
adj. Kissing the feet (of, -ke), showing respect (to); paying (one's) respects; making obeisance; — qadam-bos honā (-ke), To kiss the feet (of), &c.
s.f. Kissing the feet (of, -kī); touching the feet of the person saluted with the right hand and then kissing the latter; respectful salutation, obeisance, homage; respects
(-ke), To kiss the feet (of); to leave the company (of), to bid adieu (to)
(-ke), To touch, or to clasp, the feet (of)
(-ke), To show the paces (of); — (colloq.) to make tracks, to be off, go away
(-meṅ, or -par), To set foot (on), plant the foot (on); to step (into); to engage or embark (in), take part (in), venture (on)
v.n. To take the trouble of going or coming (to, — spoken respectfully)
adv. Step by step; at a walking or an easy pace
(with loc.), To plant the foot firmly (on); to establish or secure (one's) footing
(-ke), To cling to or clasp the feet (of); to seek the protection (of); — to have good paces, to walk well (a horse)

(-ke), To touch the feet (of); to kiss the feet (of); to pay (one's) respects (to); to bow (to), to acknowledge the superiority (of another, either in good or evil qualities; but more frequently the latter)
(-meṅ), To tread or walk (in a path or course)
(-ke), To form the paces (of), to break in (a horse)
adj. Capable of being trodden; trodden
adj. Of unlucky footsteps; unlucky, inauspicious.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قدم (qadam), from Arabic قَدَم (qadam).

ety: for A. قدامة, inf. n. of قدم 'to precede,' &c.
def: s.f. Precedence (in point of time), priority; ancientness, antiquity; age; seniority; length of service; — prescription; — excellence, worthiness.
s.m. The tree Nauclea cadamba; — an assemblage or collection, a multitude, crowd; plenty
subs: s.f. A plant (commonly called Mandīri) the flowers of which resemble those of the Kadamb.

def: s.m. A small or short step; — one step of the (brick) compartments in a necessary.
s.m. Destroying, killing, destruction, slaughter; — killer, destroyer; destructive creature.
adv. See s.v. kādā.
s.m. A saw; an elephant-goad; — a white sort of Mimosa.

ety: inf. n. of کدر 'to be turbid,' &c.
def: s.m. Being turbid or muddy; turbidness, muddiness, dirtiness; perturbation, agitation (of mind), trouble, affliction, solicitude (cf. kudūrat).
adj. & s.m. Cowardly, timid, &c.; — a coward.
s.f. Cowardice, pusillanimity, timidity, fear, &c. ( = kātaratā, q.v.).
s.f. Cowardice, pusillanimity, timidity, fear, &c. ( = kātaratā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Avaricious, miserly; mean, sordid; — a niggardly person, a miser.
s.f. = S کدريتو कदर्यत्व kad-aryatva, s.m. Avarice, miserliness, niggardliness.
s.m. = S کدريتا कदर्यता kad-aryatā, s.f. Avarice, miserliness, niggardliness.
s.f. Importuning, importunity; persistence; worry, annoyance; exertion, endeavour, diligence, trouble, labour, fatigue; examination; search; inquiry
subs: s.f. Diligent search, inquiry, examination; application, assiduity.

ety: Prk. कवड्डओ; S. कपर्दकः; cf. H. कौड़ी
def: s.m. A large cowrie (kauṛī).

r: qaddasa, quds, qaddasa sirra-hu, quddisa sirru-hu
def: 3rd pers. sing. perf. (of ii of قدس; see quds), He sanctified, he hallowed; (optatively) may he sanctify or hallow
subs: May (God) hallow his grave; — quddisa sirru-hu (passive), May his tomb be purified or hallowed (used in speaking of a deceased king or great man).

def: s.f. (dialec.) A metal pin or nail.
s.m. = kaṅḍerā, q.v.
s.m. 'The headman of a village'; an agriculturist, a farmer; a horticulturist, a gardener.

ety: inf. n. of قدح 'to revile,' &c.; 'to take (broth, &c.
def: s.m. Reviling, reproaching; reproach; ridicule; satire, a lampoon; — refuting, refutation
subs: v.t. To revile, &c.; to refute; to cross-examine, cross-question

def: s.m. (rustic) = kandhaiyā, or kanhaiyā, qq.v.
s.m. (rustic) = kandhaiyā, or kanhaiyā, qq.v.
adv. (rustic) = kad, q.v.

ety: S. कटाहः+dim. aff. lī, and nī = S. ल+ इका
def: s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk, &c. is boiled, a boiler.

ety: S. कटाहः+dim. aff. lī, and nī = S. ल+ इका
def: s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk, &c. is boiled, a boiler.
s.m. (rustic) A step-son; — adj. Exiled, banished, outcast.
s.m. (rustic) A step-son; — adj. Exiled, banished, outcast.
s.m. = kaṅḍerā, q.v.

r: kadhī, kadahiṅ, kad-hiṅ, kadahū, kadahu, kadhū, kadhūṅ, kadhīṅ, kad, hū, hī
ety: kad+ emphat. part. hū or hī, qq.v.
def: adv. = kabhī, q.v.).

r: kadhīṅ, kadahiṅ, kad-hiṅ, kadahū, kadahu, kadhū, kadhūṅ, kadhī, kad, hū, hī
ety: kad+ emphat. part. hū or hī, qq.v.
def: adv. = kabhī, q.v.).

r: kadhūṅ, kadahiṅ, kad-hiṅ, kadahū, kadahu, kadhū, kadhī, kadhīṅ, kad, hū, hī
ety: kad+ emphat. part. hū or hī, qq.v.
def: adv. = kabhī, q.v.).
adj. & adv. Ancient, old, antiquated, immemorial; archaic; — s.m. An old inhabitant, &c. ( = qadīmī)
subs: s.m. Old times, days of yore; — adj. Ancient of days; — qadīmuʼl-aiyām, or qadīmuʼl-aiyām-se, or qadīm-se, adv. Of old, from ancient times; from time immemorial; long ago; in olden times, in days of yore.

def: s.m. A crowbar; — a sweet pumpkin, Cucurbita pepo (cf. kadu).

ety: qadīm, q.v.+Zend aff. āna, or ana = S. अन
def: adv. Of old; from olden times, &c.
adj. & s.m. Old, ancient; aged; former, bygone; — an old inhabitant (of a place); — one holding by hereditary descent.

ety: act. part. of قدر 'to be able,' &c.
def: adj. Potent, powerful, mighty, able, competent, having legal power; capable, skilful
subs: (-par), To be able (to do), to be capable (of); to be provided (for).

def: adj. Powerful; — almighty; — the Almighty.
s.f. A kind of bodice.
s.m. See s.v. kad.
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat having the form of the kadam or kadamb.
s.m. Slime, mud, mire, dirt; bog; soft clay; alluvial deposit.

ety: inf. n. of قدر 'to make great; to measure, determine.' &c.
def: s.f. Greatness, dignity, honour, rank, power; importance, consequence; worth, merit; estimation, appreciation, account; value, price; — measure; degree; quantity; magnitude; bulk, size; portion, part; — whatever is fixed or ordained of God, divine providence, fate, destiny
subs: adj. & s.m. Conferring dignity or honour; — source of dignity, fountain of honour
or qadr-shinās, adj. & s.m. Knowing the worth or value (of), appreciating; — one who knows the worth (of), a just appreciator, a judge; a patron
s.f. Due regard to the worth (of), just appreciation of merit; power or faculty of appreciation; patronage, favour; — qadr-dānī karnā, or qadr karnā (-kī), To appreciate duly, to esteem, prize, value
adj. & adv. So much; to such a degree; so
adv. Ad valorem; valued at; at the rate (of, -i, or -ke); to the extent (of); to the degree (of); to the amount (of); in proportion (to); according (to), as; corresponding (with)
adj. Worthless, of no importance or account; — dishonoured, disgraced; — wanting in appreciation, insensible to merit
s.f. Indignity, dishonour, disgrace; low estimation; want of appreciation, non-recognition of merit
adj. & adv. How much? to what extent or degree? — kisī qadr, Somewhat; a little ( = qadre).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَدْر (qadr), from Arabic قَدْر (qadr).

def: s.m. & adv. A (small) quantity; a little, somewhat (of); in some measure, to some extent, in a limited degree, partially; ever so little
subs: adv. Little by little, by degrees.

def: s.m. A certain order or sect of derwishes who hold the doctrine of free will; — a member of that sect.
s.m. A pumpkin or pumpion, a gourd, the calabash, Cucurbita lagenaria; — membrum virile
subs: s.m. A disease in which the body is covered with pustules like the seeds of the kadū; — intestinal worms, Tænia cucurbitana
s.m. An implement for cutting and cleaning pumpkins, &c.

def: (rustic), adj. contrac. of kaʼī-ek, q.v.s.v. kaʼī.
s.f. = kāʼoṅ-kāʼoṅ, or kāṅw-kāṅw, q.v.

s.m. A fabulous mountain, supposed to surround the world and bound the extreme horizon (it rests on the stone saḵẖrat, an entire emerald, the reflection from which, according to the poets, occasions the azure colour of the sky; the qāf is the abode of the devs, and jinn or genii, and also of the parīs or fairies, and of the sīmurg̠ or great griffin); — Mount Caucasus
s.f. Froth, foam; scum; spittle, phlegm, mucus
subs: s.m. A skimmer, spoon, ladle (generally perforated like a colander)

def: (called qāf-ě-qarashat), the twenty-seventh letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twenty-fourth of the Persian, and twenty-first of the Arabic, alphabet). It is a strongly articulated guttural, uttered by pressing the root of the tongue on the throat, and has been compared in sound to the caw of a raven. It does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analogous letter in Sanskrit. As a numeral qāf denotes 100.

r: kāf, kāf-ě-tāzī, kāf-ě-ʻarabī, gāf, kāf-ě-ʻajamī, kāfʼ, ka, k, ka-kār
ety: called kāf-ě-tāzī, or kāf-ě-ʻarabī, 'the Arabic kāf,' to distinguish it from گ gāf, called kāf-ě-ʻajamī, 'the Persian kāfʼ
def: s.m. The letter or sound ka
subs: or ka-varg, s.m. (in Hindī Gram.), The consonants of the क ka, or guttural class (viz. क, ख, ग, घ, ङ), the guttural consonants collectively.

def: s.f. The back of the head; nape of the neck; — adv. Behind, after; in pursuit (of, -kī).
adj. Equal (to), adequate; on a par; — s.m. Sufficiency, competency, enough (to keep one from begging); pittance, daily bread.
s.f. Scum (of an indigo vat at work, &c.).

ety: dim. of kaf or kaff, q.v.
def: s.f. The part of the hand or foot stained by way of ornament.
s.m. The buttocks (of a horse, &c.), haunches, rump; crupper (of a horse); — a kind of cloth used instead of a saddle.

ety: for A. کفالة, inf. n. of کفل 'to become surety' (for
def: s.f. Surety, security, bail; responsibility; caution; a pledge, pawn
subs: or kafālat-biʼlmāl, Pecuniary bail; — kafālat-biʼn-nafs, Personal bail

ety: fr. kafn, inf. n. of کفن 'to wrap in a winding-sheet'
def: s.m. Grave-clothes, winding-sheet, shroud
subs: v.n. To exclaim or cry out in amazement, &c.; to shriek, to make a terrible noise
s.m. A stealer of grave-clothes; caitiff, miscreant
'To tie or wrap (one's) winding-sheet round the head'; to engage in a perilous adventure; to risk life; to court death.

def: s.m. A bird's cage, a cage; a coop; a lattice, grate; network; (met.) the body; the skeleton of the thorax.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَفَس (qafas), from Arabic قَفَص (qafaṣ), from Classical Syriac ܩܰܦܣܳܐ (qap̄sā), from Ancient Greek κάψα (kápsa) or Latin capsus.

def: s.m. A bird's cage, a cage; a coop; a lattice, grate; network; (met.) the body; the skeleton of the thorax.

ety: prob. kaf = kaff, q.v.+dim. aff. ća
def: s.m. A spoon, ladle, skimmer (cf. kaf-gīr); — hood (of a snake).
s.f. The hand; palm (of the hand); sole (of the foot); a handful
subs: To wring the hands with regret
s.f. The sole of the foot
s.f. Palm of the hand; — (met.) adj. Level and desert, bleak, barren (a plain, e.g. kaf-ě-dast maidān).

def: s.m. Smuggling (esp. of opium); a piece of cloth or rag steeped in the juice of the poppy and twisted into a bundle with others.
adj. & s.m. All, universal, whole, total; — the whole (of); — a tribe; a multitude
adj. Ungrateful.

ety: for A. کفارة kaffārat, fr. ii of کفر 'to cover'; 'to expiate'
def: s.m. Penitence, penance, expiation, atonement (by alms, fasting, &c.)
s.m. See s.v. kaf.

ety: for A. کاف kāfiṉ, for orig. kāfiyuṉ, act. part. of کفي 'to be sufficient'; see kifāyat
def: adj. Sufficient, enough, adequate; effectual, efficacious
s.m. A surety, security, bail, ransomer, hostage
subs: s.m. A surety, a responsible agent

ety: for A. قافلة qāfilat, v.n. fr. قفل 'to return from a journey,' &c.
def: s.m. A body of travellers or traders, &c., a caravan
subs: or qāfila-sālār, s.m. The chief of a caravan.

ety: act. part. of کفر 'to be unbelieving,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Infidel, impious; ungrateful; — one denying God, an infidel, an impious wretch; — a Caffre; (met.) a mistress, sweetheart
subs: s.f. A coat cut after the European pattern
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَافِر (kāfir), borrowed from Arabic كَافِر (kāfir).

ety: for A. کافره kāfirat, fem. of kafir
def: adj. & s.f. = kāfir.

ety: kāfir+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Infidel-like; impiously.
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to an infidel; or a Caffre; — an African Caffre, a negro; — s.f. Infidelity, unbelief; impiety; — mischief

ety: for A. قافية qāfiyat, v.n. fr. قفو 'to follow'
def: s.m. The rhyme, cadence, metre, the last letter in a verse (to which all the other distichs rhyme; in poems which terminate in a double rhyme, the penult syllable is qāfiya, the last being called radīf)
subs: (-kā), To exhaust all (one's) rhymes; — to reduce (one) to great straits; to perplex, confuse
(-kā), To be hard pressed for a rhyme; — to be hard pressed, be entangled in difficulties; to be very poor or destroyed

def: s.f. Foam; scum, &c. ( = kaf, q.v.).
v.t. To wrap in a winding-sheet, to shroud, to dress for the grave, to lay out (a corpse)
subs: v.t. To shroud and bury; to perform the funeral rites of.

def: s.f. A dress (resembling a kafan) worn by a faqīr or Mohammadan mendicant.
s.f. A sandal; slipper; shoe (high-heeled and shod with iron)
subs: or kafsh-gar, s.m. A shoe-maker; a dealer in shoes.

def: s.m. A robe of honour; a caftan.
s.m. The hyena (syn. ćarḵẖ; lakaṛ bagghā).

ety: the Arab. form of P. kāpūr = S. कर्पूर
def: s.m. Camphor
subs: v.n. To vanish, disappear, run away, decamp.

def: adj. Of camphor, made of camphor; camphorate; white as camphor, transparent white, snow-white
s.m. = kāk, q.v.
s.m. A cork ( = kāk, q.v.).

ety: Ap. Prk. कागु; Prk. कागो, and कागओ; S. काक and काक+कः
def: s.m. A crow; a raven; — kāg, s.m. The uvula
subs: (-kā), To raise the uvula (of an infant)
s.m. A kind of black pearl
(˚ga+ari), s.m. 'Enemy of the crow,' an owl
or kāg-bhusunḍ, or kāg-bhuśunḍi, s.m. = kāk-bhasunḍ, q.v.s.v. kāka
(-kā), The uvula to become elongated and hang down
s.f. Driving off crows from a field
tumult. (For other compounds, see s.v. kāk).

ety: Ap. Prk. कागु; Prk. कागो, and कागओ; S. काक and काक+कः
def: s.m. A crow; a raven; — kāg, s.m. The uvula
subs: (-kā), To raise the uvula (of an infant)
s.m. A kind of black pearl
(˚ga+ari), s.m. 'Enemy of the crow,' an owl
or kāg-bhusunḍ, or kāg-bhuśunḍi, s.m. = kāk-bhasunḍ, q.v.s.v. kāka
(-kā), The uvula to become elongated and hang down
s.f. Driving off crows from a field
tumult. (For other compounds, see s.v. kāk).

def: s.f. and m.(?) End, side, edge, border; cornice; architrave.
s.f. Oblations of food made to crows in a śrāddh.

ety: for orig. kāg̠ad; said to be fr. kāg̠, 'sound or noise'+da, 'giving forth'
def: s.m. Paper; a paper, writing, document, deed; a charter, patent; an account; a note of hand, a bond; a printed or written sheet, a newspaper
subs: s.m. Office allowance for stationery; — kāg̠aẕ-sā, adj. Paper-like; thin as paper
s.m. Government paper, a government promissory note; a currency note; registered or stamped paper
s.m. A puppet; — a thin active fellow
To prepare or make up (one's) papers; to adjust or balance (one's) accounts; — to give a note of hand or bond
s.m. (colloq.) 'Paper-horses'; active correspondence; — kāg̠aẕ-ke ghoṛe dauṛānā, To keep up an active (or a brisk) correspondence
(-ke), To open the papers (of); to expose the misdeeds (of)
(-ko), To give a written bond or note of hand (to)
(-se), To take a bond or note of hand (from)
(se), To compare papers, &c. (with); to examine or check accounts
s.m. Thick, stiff paper; — stamped paper
s.m. Inferior country paper; — unstamped paper
or be-likhā kāg̠aẕ, s.m. Blank paper.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَاغَذ (kāġaz), borrowed from Sogdian 𐼸𐼰𐼲𐼹𐼰 (kʾɣδʾ /⁠kāγaδā⁠/), possibly borrowed from Old Chinese 紙 (*kjeʔ). Compare Sindhi ڪاغذ (kāġazu).

def: s.m. pl. (of kāg̠aẕ, after the model of the reg. pl. fem. of the Ar.), Papers, documents, records, &c.

adj. & s.m. Of paper, made of paper; — paper-; paper-like, papery; thin; delicate, soft; brittle, fragile; — of or relating to papers or documents, documentary; — a paper-maker; a stationer; a writer; an accountant; — a paper case; — s.f. A superior kind of thin-peeled lemon or lime, or pear, or walnut; — a white pigeon.
adj. Made of paper.

ety: fem. of kāk or kāg, q.v.; lī = S. र or ल+इका
def: s.f. A female crow; a female raven.
s.m. (rustic) A crow; a raven ( = kāg); — black fungus which attacks cereals.
s.m. (rustic) A crow ( = kāg, q.v.).
s.f. Grass; dried grass; straw
subs: s.f. (lit. 'straw-attracting'), Yellow amber; Oriental anime, gum-resin from the Valeria indica (com. kah-rubā)
(or kah-rubāʼī), adj. Of or relating to amber; — s.f. The quality of amber; — electricity
(or kah-gil), s.f. Straw and mud mixed together for plastering walls with.

def: s.f. contrac. of kāh, 'straw,' &c., q.v.

ety: past conj. part. of kahnā: — Prk. कहे or कहिआ; S. कथित्वा, rt. कथ्
def: part. Having said, having spoken, &c. (see kahnā).

ety: rt. of kāhīdan = kāstan; see kāst
def: part. adj. Diminishing; weakening; destroying; consuming, &c. (used as last member of compounds).

ety: perf. part. of kahnā, and = Prk. कहिअओ = S. वथित+कः
def: part. Said, uttered, spoken, &c.; — s.m. Saying, word, remark, speech, discourse; call; advice; bidding, order, command; leave; affirmation, confession
subs: s.m. or kahā-sunī, s.f. Speech, words; advice, &c. ( = kahā); pleading, solicitation; — improper speech, harsh language; dispute, altercation
(-kā), To do the bidding (of), carry out the instructions (of)
s.f. Speech, words, conversation, dialogue; debate, dispute, altercation, wrangling, squabble
(-kā), To mind or heed the bidding (of), to obey.

ety: Ap. Prk. केहो, Prk. केद्दहो; S. कीट्टशः
def: pron. (old H. & Braj) What? which? how? why? (i.q. kyā, q.v.).
s.m. corr. of qahqahā, q.v.

adj. (f. -ā), Dry, withered; — large, excessive; mischievous; — s.m. A cat; a cock; — a sound (in general); — indistinct speech; — a (musical) horn; — a large drum; — kāhalā-pushp, s.m. The thorn-apple, Datura metel.
s.f. Sloth, indolence; languor (cf. kāhil).

ety: perhaps a post-classical formation from A. کہل; see kāhil
def: s.f. Inactivity, indolence, sloth, remissness.
v.n. To be parched; — to wither; — v.t. To parch (grain, partly or slightly = kahlānā).
v.t. To cause to be spoken or said; to cause to be told; — to cause to be named or called.
s.m. An aggregate number consisting of sixteen paṇs, or 1,280 cowries.
adv. Where? whither?
subs: Where? at what place? — kahāṅ-tak or talak, kahāṅ-lag, or kahāṅ-loṅ, How far? how long? to what degree?
Of or belonging to what place?
To what a degree! extremely, intensely; — from where to where; what a distance! ever so far; now here now there
Where (this) — where (that)?; how distant or how different is (this) from (that)! how little is (this) consistent with that! (e.g. kahāṅ rājā Bhoj kahāṅ Gangā telī; — cf. Pers. kujā — kujā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = kāhan, or kahāwan, qq.v.
s.m. A saying, word; a by-word; — a composition
postpn. (dialec.) = ko, the case-sign of the dat. & acc.
(dialec. & Old H. forms), postpn. = ko (the sign of the dat.-acc.), q.v.

ety: caus. of kahnā, q.v.; — the stem kahā = S. कथ+आपय, used as a substitute for S. caus. कथय˚; Prk. कहाव˚; — nā, fr. S. aff. अनीयं; Prk. अणअं
def: v.t. To cause to speak; to cause to tell, &c. ( = kahlānā, kahwānā); — v.n. To be called or named or designated, to be dubbed; to be said to be ( = kahlānā).
s.m. A tale; a saying, proverb (syn. kahāwat).
s.f. A tale, story, fable, apologue
subs: To tell a tale or story.
wety: Feminine of کَہانا (kahānā), first attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi کَہانی (khany), inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀥𑀸𑀡𑀕 (kadhāṇaga), from Sanskrit कथानक (kathānaka). Cognate with Punjabi کَہاݨی (kahāṇī) / ਕਹਾਣੀ (kahāṇī), Gujarati કહાણી (kahāṇī), Marathi कहाणी (kahāṇī), Odia କାହାଣୀ (kāhāṇi), Bengali কাহিনী (kahini), Assamese কাহিনী (kahini).

def: s.m. (prov.) = kahār, q.v.
s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose business it is to carry palanquins and the like, and to draw water, &c.; a man of that caste, a palanquin-bearer, dooley-bearer; a water-drawer, &c. (syn. dhīṅwar).
1˚ = kāhal; — 2˚ = kahār, qq.v.
s.f. A woman of the kahār caste; the wife of a kahār.
s.f. The wife of a kahār ( = kahāran); — the business of a kahār; the wages of a kahār.
s.f. A net for the load carried by kahārs and grass-cutters, &c.
v.n. To groan, &c. ( = karāhnā, q.v.).

ety: Prk. काहावणो, or कहावणो; S. कार्षापणः, or कर्षापणः
def: s.m. An aggregate number of 1280 cowries; a coin of that value ( = kāhan, and kahān).
s.f. Saying, proverb, adage.

ety: for A. قحبة qaḥbat, inf. n. of un. fr. قحب 'to cough'
def: s.m. A cough; — an old woman (subject to coughing); — a harlot, prostitute (so called because she notifies her profession by coughing); — adj. Unchaste.

ety: qaḥba, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Fornication, whoredom, prostitution.

r: kāhe, kahā, kyā, kāhe-se, kāhe-kā, kāhe-ko, kāhe-liye, kāhe-wāst̤e, kāhe-ke liye
def: the inflec. base of the Braj. pron. kahā ( = H. kyā), 'what?' — kāhe-se, 'with what?' 'from what cause?' 'wherefore?' — kāhe-kā, 'of what' (thing or material?)
subs: 'for what?' 'why?' 'wherefore?' — kāhe-liye, or kāhe-wāst̤e (for kāhe-ke liye, &c.), 'why?' 'for what reason or purpose?' 'on what ground?' &c.

def: s.m.(?) A cave, a cavern, a grotto.
part. See s.v. kahnā.
s.f. contrac. of kāh-gil, q.v.s.v. kāh.
s.m. One who anoints the eyes with antimony; an oculist.

adj. & s.m. Very powerful, mighty; of great force or impetuosity; boundless (as a large river, &c.); imperious; subduing, conquering; avenging; — a conqueror, subduer; an avenger; a tyrant, an oppressor.
s.f. Green vitriol.
adv. = kahīṅ, q.v.
adj. Of or relating to grass or straw; grassy; grass-colour, grass-green, pale-green; — s.f. Grass-colour, pale-green; greenness; — name of a dish.
s.f. Forage, provender, provision; — a foraging party; a band of marauders or plunderers.

ety: perf. part. of kāhīdan; see kāh, and kāst
def: part. Diminished; wasted; emaciated; worn; consumed; destroyed.

ety: act. part. of کہل 'to be languid,' &c.
def: adj. Languid, relaxed, lax, slow, tardy; indolent, idle, lazy, negligent; sick
subs: adj. & s.m. Indolent, lazy; — a lazy fellow.

def: adj. Ailing, sick, indisposed.

ety: kāhil, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adv. Slowly, tardily; lazily.

s.f. = H کاهلپنا kāhil-panā, s.m. [kāhil, q.v.+panā = Prk. प्पणअं = S. त्व (Vedic त्वन) +कं; and ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ), Languor; apathy; slowness, tardiness; sloth, indolence; remissness; — sickness, indisposition
subs: (-meṅ), To practise indolence, to be slothful, or remiss (in).

def: s.m. = P کاهلي kāhilī, s.f. [kāhil, q.v.+panā = Prk. प्पणअं = S. त्व (Vedic त्वन) +कं; and ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ), Languor; apathy; slowness, tardiness; sloth, indolence; remissness; — sickness, indisposition
subs: (-meṅ), To practise indolence, to be slothful, or remiss (in).

ety: act. part. of کہن 'to augur, predict,' &c.
def: s.m. Augur, soothsayer, sorcerer, magician; astrologer; a prophet; a priest.
adv. Somewhere; anywhere; wherever, whithersoever; — ever, anyhow, by any chance; ever-so-much, far, greatly; — may be, perhaps, peradventure
subs: (or aur kahīṅ), Somewhere else, elsewhere; anywhere else
adj. (f. -ī) Of or belonging to somewhere; outlandish, queer, strange
Here and there; now here now there (cf. kahāṅ-kā-kahāṅ)
In one place — in another place; now — now
Here and there, in sundry places; at times, sometimes; at intervals

def: adj. Conquering, subduing, triumphant, victorious; violent, forcible, oppressive; — qāhir, s.m. Victor, conqueror; — qāhira, s.f. Victrix; — al-qāhira, The city of Cairo (in Egypt).

ety: for A. قاهرة qāhirat, fem. of qāhir
def: adj. Conquering, subduing, triumphant, victorious; violent, forcible, oppressive; — qāhir, s.m. Victor, conqueror; — qāhira, s.f. Victrix; — al-qāhira, The city of Cairo (in Egypt).

ety: abst. s. fr. kah, rt. of kāstan; see kāh, and kāst
def: s.f. Diminution; decline, decay, wasting, emaciation, consumption; wear and tear; care, anxiety
s.m. The common or wild strawberry.
s.m. A loud laugh, &c. ( = qahqaha); — the cry of a partridge
subs: v.n. To laugh aloud, &c.; — to cry (as a partridge).

def: s.m. = qahqaha, q.v.

ety: for A. قہقہة qahqahat, inf. n. of A. قہقه 'to laugh loudly'
def: s.m. A loud laugh, a horse-laugh; a burst of laughter
subs: or qahqaha lagānā, or qahqaha lenā, or qahqaha marnā, v.n. To laugh aloud or heartily; to burst with laughter.

ety: past. conj. part. of kahnā; see kah and kar
def: part. = kah, q.v.

ety: for A. قہقرة qahqarat, inf. n. of قہقر 'to retreat'
def: s.m. Retreat; retrograde motion.
s.m. Retreat; retrograde motion.
s.m. See s.v. kāh.
s.m. = kāh-kashān, q.v.
v.t. To parch; — v.n. To wither, or wither away; — to be or become languid or weak; to be lazy (in these senses perhaps from A. kāhil, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to speak, cause to say, to cause to tell; to call; — v.n. To be called, or named, or designated, to go by the name of; to be said to be ( = kahānā)
subs: (-ko or -ke hāṅ), To send to tell, to send word or a message (to).

def: s.f. A message, word (cf. kahāwat).

ety: kah˚ = Prk. कह(इ) or कहे(इ) = S. कथय(ति), rt. कथ्;+nā = Prk. अअणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To say, utter, speak (to, -se; of, -ko); to tell, declare, mention, state, deliver, relate, repeat, recount; to acquaint (with, -se); to advise, admonish; to affirm, assert, aver, avow; to bid, order; to speak of, address, call, name, pronounce (with -ko of object); to compose; to read, to quote; — s.m. Saying, remark, a word or phrase; comment; advice; order, bidding
subs: (intens. of kahnā), To tell, &c.; to leave word with or behind
(-ke, or mere, &c.), To do as bid, to heed, mind, obey
v.t. To remark, observe; — s.m. Dispute, wrangling, altercation; — consideration; influence
(-kā), To do the bidding (of), carry out the instructions (of)
adv. For the sake of saying or talking
(-kā), To obey the order (of), to be obedient (to)
adv. Under command or control, subject to orders, submissive, obedient, in hand
or kahan-hār, s.m. Speaker, relater, narrator, &c.; (in Gram.) the first person
part. Gone to the winds, thrown away, wasted, lost (said esp. of unheeded speech, advice, instruction, &c.)
na kahūṅ to bāp kuttā khā-jāʼegā, A saying expressive of dilemma or case of difficult decision.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀥𑁂𑀤𑀺 (kadhedi), from Sanskrit कथयति (katháyati). Cognate with Assamese কোৱা (küa).

def: s.f. A saying, adage, proverb; a tale, story, fable; — style (cf. kahāwat).

ety: inf. n. of قہر 'to overcome, to conquer,' &c.
def: s.m. Force, power, violence, vehemence, severity; excess; boundlessness; oppression; subjection; rage, fury, wrath, indignation; vengeance; torment, punishment, chastisement; a judgment; a calamity; — a mischievous person, a firebrand; — adj. Vehement; excessive, very great, &c.
subs: v.n. To be wrathful, to rage; to foam
s.f. Great disturbance or commotion, terrible ado (used both in panegyric and satire)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قهر (qahr), from Arabic قَهْر (qahr).

def: adv. Violently, forcibly, perforce, &c.
v.n. = karāhnā, q.v.
s.m. = kāh-rubā, q.v.s.v. kāh.

ety: inf. n. of قحط 'to be withheld' (rain
def: s.m. Drought, scarcity, dearth; failure of the crops or harvest; famine; want, penury, hunger, starvation
subs: adj. & s.m. Stricken with dearth; famine-stricken; starving, famished; dead from starvation; — one who is starving; one who has died of starvation; a starveling; a glutton
s.f. Dearth; failure of harvest; famine.

def: s.m. A starveling; a glutton (i.q. qaḥt̤-zada, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a vegetable (the seed of which is good for a cold); a lettuce, Lactuca sativa.
adv. (Braj) = kahīṅ, q.v.
(Braj), inflec. base of koʼū, and = H. kisū, obl. of koʼī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Eloquent; talkative, garrulous; — a talker; a prater.

ety: for kāhu-kau, gen. of kāhu = koʼū, qq.v., and of kaćhū
def: adj. (dialec.), Of or relating to someone, or to something.
adv. A dialec. form of kahīṅ, q.v.
postpn. (dialec. & poetic.) = kāhaṅ, q.v.
adv. contrac. of kahūṅ, q.v.
s.m. Coffee (roasted, or roasted and ground, or the decoction)
s.m. A speaker, &c. ( = kahne-wālā).
adv. A dialec. form of kahāṅ, q.v.
v.t. To cause to speak, cause to say, cause to tell ( = kahānā, kahlānā).

ety: prob. = kāhī = S. कासीसं; — or akin to kāh, q.v.
def: s.f. Green scum on stagnant water, water-moss; green mould on walls and pavements; moss; mould; scum; fur; paste; rust
subs: v.n. To be dispersed or scattered like water-moss (clouds, or a crowd, &c.)
(with loc.), Green scum to form (on); to become covered with moss; to become mouldy, &c.

ety: Zend kadha, fr. ka; S. kadha˚
def: adv. When?

ety: Pārsī & Pehl. kai; Zend kavi; S. कवि
def: s.m. A king, or a great king (esp. a king of Persia of the Kyanian dynasty)
subs: Cyrus the Great, king of Persia
or kai-kāʼūs, 'Just or noble king'; name of a king of Persia.

def: adj. (indecl.), Several, sundry, divers; some, some few
s.f. Vomiting; vomit (syn. rad)
subs: (-ko), To feel nausea; — to be sick (of), or disgusted (with, or at, -se)
s.f. Severe bilious vomiting and purging; the cholera

def: adv. How many? — kai-bār, How many times? how often?
s.f. corr. of قي qai, q.v.
conj. Or, either
subs: Either — or; whether — or; — kai, conj. (Braj.) = ki, q.v.

def: (poet. for که اي), That O.
s.m. A leader; a general; — the last star in the tail of Ursa Minor.

s.f. A shackle, fetter, bonds; bondage; confinement, imprisonment; control; restraint; restriction; limit; condition; an obstacle; a bond, compact, treaty, bargain; rule, regulation; article (of agreement); an obligation
subs: or qaid bhugatnā, or qaid kāṭnā (-kī), To undergo the full term of imprisonment
s.m. Place of confinement, prison, gaol
v.t. To detain in confinement, to keep in custody
v.n. To remain in bonds, or in prison, &c.
s.f. A kind of fetter used by the Europeans; — imprisonment from which there is no escape
v.t. To shackle, fetter; to bind; to make captive; to imprison, &c.
(-meṅ), To apply restrictions, &c. (to); to restrict; to limit; to attach conditions (to), to make conditional (on, -kī); to subject to rule, to regulate
v.n. To be fettered, or bound; to be imprisoned; to be restricted (-to, -kī); to be limited

or bilā-qaid, adj. Unrestrained, unchecked, uncontrolled; free; unrestricted; unconditional; unlimited; indefinite; — without due regard (to or for); irregular; lawless; — be-qaid, adv. Without restraint, or opposition, or reservation; unreservedly, openly; at large, at random; irregularly.

def: s.m. Deceit, fraud; stratagem; treachery; malignity, malice; — war; — adapting, adjusting; addressing; applying (a remedy); — vomiting, casting forth; — the being menstruous (a woman); — loud croak (of a raven).

ety: for A. قاعدة qāʻidat, fem. of qāʻid, act. part. of قعد 'to sit,' &c.
def: s.m. Basis, foundation, groundwork; base (of a geom. fig., or of a column); a pedestal; (in Gram.) a rule; — rule, regulation, law, institute, precept, maxim; formula; institution; usual course of procedure, custom, usage, habit, practice, mode, manner, way; manners, etiquette; — regularity, uniformity; — a first reading-book, a primer
subs: (-kā), or qāʼida muqarrar karnā (-ko), To make a rule (of), to make (one's) established custom or practice, &c.; to regulate, fix, determine, establish; to prescribe, ordain; to provide, stipulate
s.m. One versed in rules of practice, or forms, or customs, or ceremonies; an expert, an authority
adv. According to rule or practice, as usual; in due course or form; in order; according to the rank or social condition (of, -ke).

def: s.m. A captive; a prisoner, a convict.

ety: acc. of kaifuṉ, inf. n. of کيف 'to cut,' &c.
def: adv. How? in what manner? — (met.) s.f. Hilarity, exhilaration (esp. such as is produced by drinking or chewing bhang, &c.), intoxication, partial inebriation; — an intoxicating drug.
s.f. Exhilaration, intoxication ( = kaif).
s.f. Intoxication ( = kaif).
adj. & s.m. Exhilarated, merry, tipsy, intoxicated, half-drunk; — a tipsy person, a drunkard.

ety: for A. کيفية kaifīyat, abst. s. fr. kaifa
def: s.f. Quality, nature, character; mode, state, condition, circumstances; account, statement, remarks, report, particulars; relation, story, news; — exquisite state, flourishing state, enjoyableness, deliciousness, exquisite enjoyment; a sight to be seen, a beautiful view, &c.
subs: (-kī), To prepare an account or a report (of); to draw out a schedule (of), &c.
(-kī, of thing, and -se, from a person), To call for information, or explanation, or report
A place of exquisite enjoyment, a beautiful or charming spot.

def: s.m. A flea (syn. pissū).

ety: act. part. of قول 'to say; to speak,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Saying; confessing, acknowledging, owning; consenting, agreeing, acquiescing; giving up a point; being convinced, or confuted, or convicted; — a sayer, an assertor; a confessor, &c.
subs: (-ko), To confute; to convince; to convict
(-kā), To be convinced (of); to acquiesce (in); to acknowledge, confess, own; to consent, assent, yield; to be confuted; to be silenced.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَائِل (qā'il), from Arabic قَائِل (qāʔil).

def: s.f. A sprout, a shoot (cf. kair).
s.m. A measure of grain, as well as of other dry goods and liquids.
s.m. = kailās, q.v.

s.m. Name of a mountain, placed by the Hindūs in the Himālaya range (it is regarded as one of the loftiest peaks to the north of the Mānasa lake; and is fabled as the residence of Kuvera and the favourite haunt of Śiva).

ety: for A. قيلولة qailūlat, v.n. fr. قيل 'to sleep at mid-day'
def: s.m. Mid-day nap, siesta.
(fr. Gr. χιλὸς), s.m.(?) Chyle.

adj. Standing; erect, perpendicular; right; — stationary; stagnant; quiescent; firm, fixed, established; durable, lasting, perpetual, constant; stable; stedfast; — continuing, in force; — vigilant; attentive, persevering; — stopped, drawn (a game); — s.f. A drawn game (of chess, &c.)
subs: s.m.(?), lit. 'Fixed by fire'; fixed quicksilver
(-par), To keep (one) firm or fixed (to); to keep up, to preserve; to make good; to uphold, support

(-par), To continue, remain; to hold or stand good, to continue in operation or force; to survive, outlive; to stand firm, to keep (one's) footing; to keep constant or stedfast (to), to continue firm (in); to stick (to)
v.t. To erect; to set up (a claim, or right, &c.); to fix, establish, institute; to constitute; to appoint (to, -par), to instal (in); to render permanent, &c.; to uphold, maintain, support
adj. Of a settled temper or mind, fixed, constant; firm, determined, resolute; persevering

s.m. One who acts in the place (of another), a locum-tenens; a viceroy, vicegerent, lieutenant, deputy, representative; successor; assignee; surrogate; procurator; proctor; — adj. Acting, or officiating
s.f. Vicegerency, &c.; succession; assigneeship; representation; deputation

(-par), To be erected (on); to be set up; to be established, to be instituted; to be fixed or settled (in); to be firm (in); to be permanently appointed (to); — to stand (upon); to stand or hold (to); — to arise, spring up; to reveal or manifest itself; to take place, happen, occur.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَائِم (qā'im), from Arabic قَائِم (qāʔim).

ety: for A. قائمة qāʼimat, fem. of qāʼim
def: s.m. A perpendicular; a right angle.
s.f. Durability, fixity, permanence, &c.
(fr. Gr. χυμὸς), s.m.(?) The chyme.
s.f. A bamboo twig.
(Dakh.), adv. corr. of kahīṅ, q.v.

ety: pl. of kāʼinat, n. of un. fr. kāʼin, act. part. of کان (for کون
def: s.f. Existing things, existences, beings, creatures; the universe, the world; — property, possessions, means, stock, capital; — worth, value.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کائنات (kâ'inât), itself from Arabic كَائِنَات (kāʔināt), the plural form of كَائِن (kāʔin).

def: s.f. Scissors; — an oblique, or St. Andrew's cross; — the joists of a roof); a truss; — a rostrum
subs: v.n. (in wrestling), To twine the legs (round, -par), to bring an adversary between one's legs; to bind or fasten (an unskilful rider, on his horse)
v.t. To cut (the hair, &c.), to clip, lop, prune
(-meṅ), To apply scissors (to), to cut (cloth, &c.); to apply, or to make, a truss.

def: s.f. corr. of qainćī, q.v.
s.m. Rough measurement; a rough plan; — a mould; a sample; — a gauge
s.m. corr. of karīr, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), 'Karīr-coloured;' light-blue, grey (eyes, &c.); wall-eyed; — squint-eyed (in this sense, perhaps, from S. केकरः, or केदरः)
subs: s.f. Light-blue eye, or grey eye; a wall-eye.

def: adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to the Kirātas; — a prince of the Kirātas.
adj. (f. -ī), Relating or belonging to the Kirāta people.
s.m. A species of vegetable poison, one of the fixed kinds.
s.m. A gambler; a cheat, rogue; an enemy; — the white esculent water-lily or lotus, Nymphæa lotus.
s.f. A small unripe mango; — a locket or ornament made in the shape of a mango (used to keep perfume, &c. in); — an artificial flower.
prop. n. Name of a celebrated Arabian lover.
adj. (f. -ī), & adv. What-like? what sort of? what kind or manner of?
subs: Of whatsoever sort; in whatsoever manner; howsoever; how much soever; — kaisā-hī-taisā-hī, However much — still

def: adv. contrac. of kaisā-hī, q.v.s.v. kaisā.

ety: Prk. कइसिल्लो, or केरिसिल्लो = S. कीट्टश+इलः
def: dialec. forms of kaisā, q.v.
(Gr. Καῖσαρ; Lat. Cœsar), s.m. & f. Cæsar; an emperor; an empress; — the general name for the Greek or Turkish emperors.
adj. & adv. = kaisā, q.v.
adv. = kaisā, q.v.
adv. = kaisā, q.v.
adj. & adv. = kaisā, q.v.
s.m. Southernwood, Artemisia abrotanum.
s.m. 1˚ = kaith, q.v.; — 2˚ = kaithā, q.v.
s.m. Name of an Asur or demon (slam by Vishnu)
subs: 'conqueror of Kaiṭabh,' and kaiṭabh-han, 'slayer of Kaiṭabh'; epithets of Vishnu.

def: s.m. The stake (in a game); gambling; — cheating, roguery; deceit, fraud, treachery; — lapis lazuli.

ety: Prk. कइत्थो, and कइत्थं; S. कपित्यः, and कपित्यं
def: s.m. The wood-apple tree, Feronia elephantum; — the fruit of this tree, the wood-apple.
s.m. An intoxicating drink made from the kaith or wood-apple.

ety: Prk. कइत्थो, and कइत्थं; S. कपित्यः, and कपित्यं
def: s.m. The wood-apple tree, Feronia elephantum; — the fruit of this tree, the wood-apple.
s.f. = kāyathī, q.v. (syn. muṛiyā)

ety: also used in Egyptian and Syrian
def: s.f. A border, edging (of silk, &c.); a kind of lace.

s.m. Eternal emancipation (i.e. the release of the soul from further emancipation); complete absorption in the Divine essence; absolute and eternal happiness, beatitude; — exclusiveness, soleness, individuality, totality.

ety: see kai, and cf. Zend kavan
def: s.m. The planet Saturn (which is said to be in the seventh sphere or heaven); — the seventh heaven.
adj. Of or relating to Saturn.
s.m. A fisherman ( = kewaṭ, q.v.); a particular mixed tribe; the son of a Nishād by an Ayogavī woman.
s.m. A measurer of grain; a weighman.
s.f. A weighman's fee or perquisite for weighing or measuring grain.
adj. Standing; erect; — true; — s.m. Nature.
adj. Permanent, lasting; everlasting; stable, fixed, steady; — an epithet of the deity.
s.m. A mode of tying a horse's head by passing the bridle to his tail (to prevent his biting, &c. while being rubbed down); — a bridle used whilst cleaning a horse, a bit or curb
subs: v.t. To tie a horse's head by passing the bridle to the tail.

def: s.f. A bridle, &c. ( = qāʼiza, q.v.).
s.m. A button-hole.

ety: or kazh, or (in old P.) gaz or gazh; akin to S. वृजन, rt. वृज्
def: adj. Crooked, curved, bent, wry; — perverse, cross-grained; cross
subs: adj. & s.m. Playing unfairly; fraudulent; — one who plays unfairly or false
adj. & s.m. Reasoning absurdly, using crooked arguments; — one who reasons absurdly, &c.
s.f. Absurd or tortuous reasoning; irregular debate; cross questions and crooked answers
adj. & s.m. Squint-eyed; — a squint-eyed person
adj. & s.m. Ill-tempered; rude, rough; — an ill-tempered person, &c.
or kaj-dār-marez, s.m. Evasion, subterfuge, putting off, commanding and countermanding, shilly-shally
adj. Crooked in mind, perverse, ill-natured
s.m. lit. 'Having a crooked tail'; a scorpion; a dragon
adj. Having a wrong opinion; wrong-headed
or kaj-rau, adj. & s.m. Walking crookedly or obliquely; — perverse; unprincipled; irregular; — one who walks obliquely, &c.
s.f. Walking obliquely; — tortuous conduct, perverseness, unprincipled conduct, depravity
adj. & s.m. Cross-tempered, cross-grained, of a crooked nature, perverse; not straightforward, unprincipled, dishonest; — a cross-tempered person, &c.
adj. Stupid, wrong-headed, perverting a meaning, perverse
s.f. Obliquity of mind, stupidity, wrong-headedness, perverseness
s.m. One who wears his cap awry; a fop, a beau (syn. bāṅkā)
adj. Athwart and across; distorted; — s.f. Distorted speech or language; a jargon
the imperfect language of a child
adj. & s.m. Cross, ill-tempered perverse; stupid
adj. Scowling, casting malignant looks; envious, malignant

ety: Prk. कज्जं and कज्जअं; S. कार्यं, and कार्य+कं
def: s.m. Work, action, occupation, business, an affair; — a feast, an entertainment (esp. one given on the death of an old person); — kāje (obl. of kājā, or S. kārye), postpn. For the purpose (of, -ke); by reason (of), on account (of); for the sake (of).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀚𑁆𑀚 (kajja), from Sanskrit कार्य (kāryà).

ety: Prk. कज्जं and कज्जअं; S. कार्यं, and कार्य+कं
def: s.m. Work, action, occupation, business, an affair; — a feast, an entertainment (esp. one given on the death of an old person); — kāje (obl. of kājā, or S. kārye), postpn. For the purpose (of, -ke); by reason (of), on account (of); for the sake (of).
adv. corr. or kadāćit, q.v.
s.f. An elephant-goad (syn. aṅkus).
s.m. Lamp-black (applied medicinally and as a collyrium to the eyes); soot
subs: (with loc.), To collect lamp-black (on a plate, &c., by placing it over a lamp or candle)
s.m. A box or receptacle for lamp-black
or kājal sārnā = kājal lagānā, q.v.

s.f. A room in which lamp-black is stored; a coal-cellar; a pitch-pot, &c.; — a place the going into which, or an affair the being engaged in which, brings disgrace or a stain or suspicion on one's character
